purpose: Bidirectional Claude ⇄ Codex permission sync. Reads/writes ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.codex/config.toml + ~/.codex/rules/default.rules; generalizes captured commands (e.g. `Bash(git log -5)` → `Bash(git *)`); filters destructive removal commands; manages Codex trust level, tri-state sandbox mode (workspace-write / danger-full-access / off) plus the [sandbox_workspace_write] network_access section, full-auto alias, the global approval_policy, a platform sandbox-runtime probe (bwrap on Linux/WSL2), and a cloud-managed-requirements probe that reads ~/.codex/cloud-requirements-cache.json. Includes a cloud-shadow filter (extractCloudShadowedFirstTokens + getCloudShadowedFirstTokens + countCloudShadowedAllow) so deriveSafeCommandsFromAllow and claudeAllowToCodexRules can drop allow entries whose argv[0] is force-prompted by an active cloud rule (would no-op anyway since the engine takes max over matches and Prompt > Allow).
invariants: Removal-command filter (rm, del, rmdir, erase, rd …) runs before generalization — destructive commands cannot be hidden behind wildcards.
Codex prefix_rule grammar rejects shell metacharacters (|&;<>$`), env-var prefixes, redirections, heredocs, and wildcards in tokens.

applyCodexSandboxMode does verify-and-revert: reads the file back after writing and reverts on any mismatch or I/O error. Mode is tri-state: 'workspace-write' | 'danger-full-access' | null (null removes the line).
applyCodexSandboxNetworkAccess strips the entire [sandbox_workspace_write] section before re-emitting it — re-enable is idempotent and never duplicates the section.
setCodexApprovalPolicy unconditionally strips the legacy [approval_policy.granular] section — current Codex CLI rejects it as a non-unit variant.
setCodexApprovalPolicy(null) only removes the line when its current value is 'never' or 'untrusted' (the values we manage); user-set values like 'on-request' are preserved.

deriveApprovalPolicyForSandboxModes prefers 'never' over 'untrusted' and consults probeCloudRequirements() to detect cloud-managed caps; under cloud cap, workspace-write also yields 'untrusted' for QoL (cloud would downgrade 'never' to 'on-request' anyway).
consolidatePermissionsToGlobal promotes any perm appearing in ≥ 2 contexts to ~/.claude/settings.json and removes it from per-context stores.
gotchas: isRuleSafeCommand rejects commands > 240 chars — long pipelines cannot be safe-commanded.
tokenizeCommand preserves quote marks within tokens; quote stripping is the caller's job.
claudeAllowToCodexRules outright rejects bare `Bash(*)` (too dangerous); strips a single trailing wildcard but rejects wildcards mid-pattern.
extractCodexTrust matches `[projects."<root>"]` headers exactly — small whitespace differences cause it to insert a duplicate section.
applyCodexSandboxMode operates on per-project .codex/config.toml — NOT the global ~/.codex/config.toml — scope confusion is a recurring bug source.
probeSandboxRuntime returns ok=true on macOS and Windows unconditionally; only Linux/WSL2 actually scans PATH for bwrap. /proc/version sniff classifies wsl2 vs linux.

probeCloudRequirements parses TOML fields out of the JSON envelope's signed_payload.contents with regex (no TOML lib). Returns null for personal/unmanaged Codex installs (no cache file). expired flag is set when expires_at is in the past, but caller decides what to do — payload is not auto-discarded.

extractCloudShadowedFirstTokens only mines the first pattern element of each [[rules.prefix_rules]] block — cloud rules block argv[0] and later positions are positional refinements. forbidden decisions are also shadowing (they too defeat a local Allow via max-of-matches). getCloudShadowedFirstTokens returns null if the cache is expired so callers don't filter against stale policy.
claudeAllowToCodexRules and deriveSafeCommandsFromAllow both accept an optional shadowedFirstTokens Set; null/missing means no filter (legacy callers unchanged).
